title: schoenorchis scolopendria
intro: Schoenorchis scolopendria, also known as the Snake Flower or Adder's Tongue, is a type of orchid plant native to tropical regions of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are said to aid in treating various health issues. The health benefits associated with Schoenorchis scolopendria include relief from fever, sore throat, and skin infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Schoenorchis scolopendria are typically made by boiling the plant's leaves and roots in water to create a tea or by drying the plant to create a powder for consumption.
